title: Genmab — Overview
html:
Genmab — Account Overview

Source: Dave Johnson FY26 AO Plan (Apr 2026 update) AO: Dave Johnson (Principal) AD: Melissa Weinstein FY25 Base: $1.34M FY26 Target: $1.47M (10% growth) FY26 YTD (3/31/26): $482K (33% of target)

Account Strategy

Core approach: Renew Epkinly PET + support all aspects of Rina-S launch. Continue depth of partnership through account transition from Brooke to Dave/Melissa.

1H26 Milestones:

  • Achieved full renewal for Epkinly PET
  • Account restructuring underway — RINA-S lead moved to marketing role (awaiting replacement); Epkinly leadership potentially changing — impacting BD in March/April
  • Epkinly approved as first bispecific in lymphoma for combo therapy (Nov '25) — expansion opportunity
  • RINA-S approval for ovarian/endometrial anticipated late 2026

Financial gaps:

  • Current bookings: $482K
  • Weighted pipeline: $270K
  • Gap to target: $718K
  • Focused on closing gap in 2H26 with MSL renewals, Rapid Turn, digital tracking, pre-launch RINA-S opportunities

Key Relationships

  • Luan Dao — Director over all Genmab
  • Angela Sun — Director for AbbVie co-promote (on 6-month marketing rotation)

Org & Competitive Context

  • ZoomRx has good partnership with Genmab
  • AbbVie co-promote on Epkinly has challenges but hasn't slowed opportunities
  • Recent acquisition of Merus has potential to create new opportunities
  • $500K of FY25 work was non-repeatable — FY26 growth needs new wins to offset

AI Status

  • Unaware of AI advancement beyond capabilities
  • General capabilities introduced; focused on this with account leader changes
  • At capabilities stage, no deeper AI discussions

Key Brands

Brand Status Notes
Epkinly (lymphoma) Core PET renewed. First bispecific approved for combo therapy (Nov '25). MSL renewals + digital tracking in pipeline
RINA-S (ovarian/endometrial) Pre-launch Approval anticipated late 2026. Pre-launch opportunities being discussed

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
